speaker: AENEAS
text: Stand, ho! Yet are we masters of the field.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Never go home; here starve we out the night.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hector is slain.

speaker: ALL
text: Hector! The gods forbid!

speaker: TROILUS
text: He’s dead, and at the murderer’s horse’s tail,

speaker: TROILUS
text: In beastly sort, dragged through the shameful field.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Frown on, you heavens; effect your rage with speed.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Sit, gods, upon your thrones, and smite at Troy!

speaker: TROILUS
text: I say at once: let your brief plagues be mercy,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And linger not our sure destructions on!

speaker: AENEAS
text: My lord, you do discomfort all the host.

speaker: TROILUS
text: You understand me not that tell me so.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I do not speak of flight, of fear, of death,

speaker: TROILUS
text: But dare all imminence that gods and men

speaker: TROILUS
text: Address their dangers in. Hector is gone.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Who shall tell Priam so, or Hecuba?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let him that will a screech-owl aye be called

speaker: TROILUS
text: Go into Troy and say their Hector’s dead.

speaker: TROILUS
text: There is a word will Priam turn to stone,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Make wells and Niobes of the maids and wives,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cold statues of the youth and, in a word,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Scare Troy out of itself. But march away.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hector is dead. There is no more to say.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Stay yet. You vile abominable tents,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Thus proudly pitched upon our Phrygian plains,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let Titan rise as early as he dare,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I’ll through and through you! And, thou great-sized coward,

speaker: TROILUS
text: No space of earth shall sunder our two hates.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I’ll haunt thee like a wicked conscience still,

speaker: TROILUS
text: That moldeth goblins swift as frenzy’s thoughts.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Strike a free march to Troy! With comfort go.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hope of revenge shall hide our inward woe.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: But hear you, hear you!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hence, broker, lackey! Ignomy and shame

speaker: TROILUS
text: Pursue thy life, and live aye with thy name!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: A goodly medicine for my aching bones! O

speaker: PANDARUS
text: world, world, world! Thus is the poor agent despised.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: O traitors and bawds, how earnestly are

speaker: PANDARUS
text: you set a-work, and how ill requited! Why should

speaker: PANDARUS
text: our endeavor be so loved and the performance so

speaker: PANDARUS
text: loathed? What verse for it? What instance for it?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Let me see:

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Full merrily the humble-bee doth sing,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Till he hath lost his honey and his sting;

speaker: PANDARUS
text: And being once subdued in armèd tail,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Sweet honey and sweet notes together fail.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Good traders in the flesh, set this in your painted

speaker: PANDARUS
text: cloths:

speaker: PANDARUS
text: As many as be here of panders’ hall,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Your eyes, half out, weep out at Pandar’s fall;

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Or if you cannot weep, yet give some groans,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Though not for me, yet for your aching bones.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Brethren and sisters of the hold-door trade,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Some two months hence my will shall here be made.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: It should be now, but that my fear is this:

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Some gallèd goose of Winchester would hiss.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Till then I’ll sweat and seek about for eases,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: And at that time bequeath you my diseases.
